explanation: The LTL formula describes a scenario where prop_0 becomes true until prop_1, under the condition that prop_2 is true, prop_3 holds unless prop_4 becomes true. Also, the formula uses the 'releases' operator to ensure that if prop_4 eventually becomes true, prop_3 must always hold.
The formula states that prop_0 eventually holds, and it holds as long as prop_1 holds, and prop_2 and prop_3 hold.
The LTL formula states that prop_0 becomes true when prop_2 and prop_3 are true and standby is not true.  It then releases from apfail.
The LTL formula states that prop_1 is true whenever prop_2 & !prop_3 implies prop_0 is true.
The LTL formula states that prop_1 must eventually be true if prop_0 and prop_2 and prop_3 are simultaneously true.
The LTL formula expresses that prop_1 holds whenever prop_0 is true and prop_2 is true and prop_3 is true. It uses the implication operator to relate the antecedent to the consequent.

The LTL formula states that prop_0 becomes prop_1 when prop_2 is true and prop_3 is true without any failures indicated by prop_4.

The LTL formula translates the natural language statement into a formal language that specifies the desired behavior. It ensures that prop_0 becomes true when prop_2 and prop_3 hold, without any occurrences of prop_4. The formula uses 'U' (until) to specify that prop_3 should hold until prop_4 becomes true, and '->' (implication) to state that the combination of prop_2 and prop_3 should lead to prop_0.
The LTL formula states that prop_0 holds if prop_1 holds, and whenever prop_2 and prop_3 are true. It uses the implication operator to express the condition.
The formula states that prop_1 must hold after prop_0 whenever prop_2 and prop_3 are true.

The requirement states that prop_0 should become true when prop_2 is true and prop_3 is true while prop_4 is false. We use a combination of boolean operators and the G (globally) and -> (implication) operators to express this. The final formula is G((prop_2 & prop_3 & !prop_4) -> (prop_0 -> prop_1)).
The LTL formula expresses that prop_0 becomes prop_1 when prop_2 and prop_3 hold without any violation of prop_4.

The LTL formula states that prop_0 becomes true eventually if prop_2 is true and prop_3 is true as long as prop_4 remains false. It uses the 'releases' operator to ensure that prop_1 holds whenever prop_2 and prop_3 are true, except when prop_4 is true.
The LTL formula states that prop_0 holds until prop_1 holds, but prop_2 and prop_3 are always true while prop_0 is true without failures (prop_4).

The LTL formula states that prop_0 is true until prop_1 when the prop_2 and the prop_3 are false. The formula is equivalent to: G((!prop_2 & !prop_3) -> (prop_0 U prop_1)).

The formula states that prop_0 shall become prop_1 when prop_2 is true and prop_3 is true until prop_4 becomes true.  The formula should use releases operator as it is a 'without failures' statement.

The LTL formula states that prop_1 must eventually hold true after prop_2 is true and prop_3 is true and prop_4 is false, and prop_0 holds.

The given natural language statement is translated to LTL as follows: G(prop_2 & !standby & prop_3 V !prop_4 -> (prop_0 -> prop_1)). This LTL formula uses releases operator and ensures that prop_0 implies prop_1 when prop_2 is true, not in standby, and prop_3 releases not apfail.
